explanations.21: This line tells Docker to create a named volume called 'limesurvey' and mount it inside the container at /var/www/html/upload/surveys, so survey data persists even if the container restarts.
explanations.33: This line does the same thing for the database: it creates a 'data' volume and mounts it at /var/lib/mysql, so the database files aren't lost when the container stops.
explanations.41: This top-level 'volumes' section defines the named volumes that both containers will use; without this declaration, the volume mounts in the services above wouldn't work.
